title: lithocarpus quercifolius
intro: Lithocarpus quercifolius, also known as the Bur oak or Quercifolius, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Lithocarpus quercifolius has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant's herbal preparations include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
